requirements.text: `.planning/current-activity.json` is written atomically at every major workflow state transition (execute-phase, plan-phase, debug, quorum, circuit-breaker, new-milestone stages)

Activity schema: `{ activity, sub_activity, phase?, plan?, wave?, debug_round?, checkpoint?, quorum_round?, updated }` — all fields except `activity` and `updated` are optional; unknown fields are preserved
`gsd-tools.cjs activity-set <json>` CLI command writes the current-activity.json file with atomic replace; `activity-clear` removes it
`resume-work` reads current-activity.json and routes to the exact recovery point — displaying the interrupted state before resuming execution
`execute-phase` writes activity at every stage boundary: entering/exiting plan execution, checkpoint:verify, debug loop rounds, awaiting_human_verify, and verifying_phase
`plan-phase`, `new-milestone`, `debug`, `quorum`, and `circuit-breaker` resolution workflows write activity at every stage boundary
Activity file is cleared on successful completion of any top-level workflow; it persists across context resets when mid-workflow
User can add a new claude-mcp-server instance (name, provider, model, key)
User can remove an existing agent from the roster
Wizard runs identity ping to verify connectivity after provisioning new agent

Subagents spawned by QGSD skills MUST loop autonomously until a terminal condition is reached. Valid terminal conditions: (1) success — the goal is verifiably met, (2) iteration cap exhausted (default max 5), (3) unrecoverable — all available paths are low-confidence or unavailable. Returning a message instructing the user to "run again" or "re-invoke" instead of looping is a violation.
All 11 TLA+ model files contain `@requirement` structured comments on each property/invariant, mapping it to specific requirement IDs
All 8 Alloy model files contain `@requirement` structured comments on each assertion/check, mapping it to specific requirement IDs
All 3 PRISM .props files contain `@requirement` structured comments on each property, mapping it to specific requirement IDs
`bin/extract-annotations.cjs` parses `@requirement` comments from TLA+, Alloy, and PRISM files and returns a structured JSON map of `{ model_file: [{ property, requirement_ids }] }`
The traceability matrix generator reads extracted annotations as a primary data source, with model-registry `requirements` arrays as fallback
All formal models declared in `.planning/formal/model-registry.json` as single source of truth with provenance tracking
`promote-model.cjs` provides atomic promotion from per-phase specs to canonical specs with duplicate detection
`accept-debug-invariant.cjs` writes debug-discovered invariants directly to canonical specs with session provenance
QGSD, as a Claude Code plugin, MUST NOT bundle LLM SDKs; Haiku/Sonnet/Opus calls MUST use the Agent tool's model parameter
`.planning/formal/policy.yaml` is the single authoritative source for PRISM calibration parameters
`run-prism.cjs` reads `policy.yaml` and writes `writeCheckResult` with `observation_window` metadata
`read-policy.cjs` exposes all policy fields with typed interface
`policy.conservative_priors.tp_rate` and `policy.conservative_priors.unavail` from `.planning/formal/policy.yaml` wire directly to `run-prism.cjs` fallback constants (v0.19-10 fix)
Automated test suite runs on every pull request and merge to main is blocked when tests fail
Linting and formatting checks run in CI and block merge on violations
Type checking (if applicable to the language) runs in CI and blocks merge on type errors

Full-suite test fallback enumerates files via find (never raw globs), enforces --test-timeout=15000 per file and a 5-minute Bash timeout, and treats timeout as pass-with-warning when task-specific tests already passed.
requirements.background: Raw glob patterns passed to node --test cause zsh fatal errors when no files match, and the 373+ test file suite exceeds the default 2-minute Bash timeout, leading to hangs and retry loops. This requirement codifies find-based enumeration and layered timeouts as mandatory patterns in the quick workflow.

requirements.text: User can define a `quorum.active` array in `qgsd.json` listing which slots participate in quorum
Quorum orchestrator reads `quorum.active` from config instead of a hardcoded agent list; only active slots are called
`check-provider-health.chs` and scoreboard tooling derive agent list from `quorum.active` rather than hardcoded arrays
Default `quorum.active` is auto-populated at install/migration time based on discovered slots in `~/.claude.json`
Global config at `~/.claude/qgsd.json` — installed once, applies to all projects
Per-project override at `.claude/qgsd.json` — merged with global, project values take precedence
Config contains: `quorum_commands` (array of command names), `required_models` (dict of MCP tool entries: { tool_prefix, required }), `fail_mode` (open|closed, default: open)
Fail-open behavior: when a quorum model is unavailable, Stop hook passes and logs reduced quorum notification
Config validates on read — malformed config falls back to hardcoded defaults with warning
qgsd.json schema extended with `circuit_breaker.oscillation_depth` (integer, default: 3) — minimum commits touching same file set to trigger breaker
qgsd.json schema extended with `circuit_breaker.commit_window` (integer, default: 6) — number of recent commits to analyze
Circuit breaker config values validated on load; invalid values fall back to defaults with stderr warning
Two-layer config merge (global + project) applies to `circuit_breaker` settings identically to existing merge behavior
Configuration layer validates and gracefully degrades when providers.json contains zero providers, falling back to solo quorum mode with clear diagnostic messages
requirements.background: Empty roster (no MCP providers configured) previously caused undefined property access crashes in quorum dispatch, TUI scoreboard rendering, and probe tooling. This requirement ensures the system detects empty provider arrays at multiple entry points and provides safe fallback behavior with actionable error messages, making misconfiguration scenarios debuggable and enabling graceful single-agent operation.

requirements.text: Global polyrepo registry at ~/.claude/polyrepos/<name>.json and per-repo marker at .planning/polyrepo.json enable named groups of related repositories with cross-repo awareness support

Per-repo marker at .planning/polyrepo.json supports an optional `docs` field with merge-semantic key-value pairs (user, developer, examples, or custom) identifying documentation and example locations relative to the repo root

Config audit script (bin/config-audit.cjs) cross-references providers.json against nf.json agent_config, detects the all-default auth_type=api anti-pattern that defeats FALLBACK-01 tiered quorum fallback, outputs structured JSON warnings, and is invoked fail-open in solve Step 0b.
requirements.background: When agent_config is empty, all quorum slots default to auth_type=api, making the T1 (subscription-tier) set permanently empty and silently defeating FALLBACK-01 tiered fallback. The config audit detects this anti-pattern at solve startup and warns the operator, with a regression test (TC-PROMPT-FALLBACK-EMPTY-AGENTCONFIG) proving the simple failover rule is used instead of the tiered sequence.

requirements.text: User can rotate API keys across multiple slots in a single batch flow from the main menu
Key validity status persists to `qgsd.json` after each health probe (enables DISP-03 badge to survive across sessions without re-probing)
Account and credential management modules (account-manager, CCR secure config/start, GitHub account rotation) SHALL have @requirement annotations and test tracing.
requirements.background: 4 credential management modules lack requirement tracing. Extends the existing CRED-* prefix to cover the full account management toolchain.

requirements.text: User can open a live health dashboard from main menu showing all slots' provider, model, and health status
Dashboard refreshes on keypress (space / r) with a visible "last updated" timestamp shown at bottom
Dashboard exits cleanly on Q or Escape, returning to main menu with stdin fully restored (no character-swallowing)

The debt/observability subsystem (debt ledger, dedup, retention, state machine, drift fingerprinting) SHALL maintain requirement traceability via @requirement annotations in source and @req annotations in tests.
requirements.background: 7 debt/observability modules and their tests lack requirement tracing. This generalized requirement covers the entire debt subsystem rather than creating per-module requirements.

requirements.text: `bin/analyze-state-space.cjs` reads TLA+ .cfg files and model variables to estimate state-space size per model and classifies risk as MINIMAL/LOW/MODERATE/HIGH based on configurable thresholds
The state-space analyzer flags models using unbounded domains (Nat, Int without constraints) as HIGH risk
When a model is split into sub-models, the traceability matrix validates that no requirement loses coverage (pre-split coverage >= post-split coverage for every affected requirement)
The state-space analysis report is included in the traceability matrix output as a `state_space` section per model

`analyze-state-space.cjs` identifies model pairs sharing source files or requirement prefixes, estimates the merged state space, and recommends merge when combined TLC runtime < 5 minutes — or flags the interface contract needed when merge would exceed the time budget
requirements.background: If a bug or instability can cross the boundary of two models, those models should be merged — unless the merged model would be computationally infeasible (>5 min TLC budget). When merge is infeasible, explicit assume-guarantee interface contracts are required between the models.

requirements.text: PreToolUse hook intercepts Bash tool calls and checks whether the current context has an active circuit breaker before running detection
Hook retrieves last N commits' changed files via `git log --name-only` (N = commit_window config) when detection is needed

Hook identifies oscillation when the exact same file set (strict set equality, not intersection) appears in ≥ oscillation_depth of the last commit_window commits — strict equality prevents false positives during TDD cycles where different files are touched per commit
Read-only Bash commands (git log, git diff, grep, cat, ls, head, tail, find) pass through without detection or blocking
Detection is skipped (returns pass) when no git repository exists in the current working directory

Oscillation detection requires at least one diff pair with net content removal (deletions > additions) before classifying a commit pattern as oscillation — pure zero-net substitution pairs are not treated as reversion evidence
requirements.background: The hasReversionInHashes function previously used totalNetChange <= 0 as the sole oscillation signal, causing false positives during monotonic workflow progression (e.g., template placeholders replaced by real data). Adding a hasNegativePair guard ensures only true content reversions trigger the circuit breaker.

requirements.text: qgsd-prompt.js runs a fast health probe (<3s) per provider before building the dispatch list -- dead providers' slots excluded from DISPATCH_LIST
qgsd-prompt.js reads scoreboard `availability` windows and excludes slots whose `available_at` is in the future from dispatch
Dispatch list ordered by recent success rate (from scoreboard slot stats) rather than static FALLBACK-01 tier sequence -- most reliable slots dispatched first

Prompt construction (Mode A/B, Round 1/2+, artifact injection, prior_positions, review_context, improvements request) happens deterministically in JavaScript (call-quorum-slot.cjs or wrapper), not via LLM string manipulation in the slot worker agent

Output parsing (verdict, reasoning, citations, improvements extraction from raw CLI output) happens in JavaScript with structured YAML output -- the slot worker agent returns script output verbatim without LLM post-processing

Quorum dispatch prompts inject a matched subset of formal requirements (from .planning/formal/requirements.json) based on keywords extracted from the question and artifact path, enabling agents to evaluate changes against concrete acceptance criteria.
requirements.background: Quorum agents previously lacked knowledge of the 237 formal requirements and could not verify whether proposed changes satisfy specific acceptance criteria. By injecting a keyword-matched subset (capped at 20) into dispatch prompts, agents gain concrete context for evaluating design decisions against project constraints.

requirements.text: README.md capability claims (setup wizard, verification workflow, adaptive fan-out, model registry, branch config, CLI commands) SHALL be backed by traceable requirements in requirements.json.
requirements.background: 13 README.md claims describe user-facing capabilities without requirement backing. This generalized requirement ensures documentation claims are traceable to formal requirements rather than creating per-claim entries.

requirements.text: README.md Formal Verification section presents user-facing capabilities (what is guaranteed, how to run verification) without exposing internal model names, directory trees, or CI pipeline details
requirements.background: The original section listed 25+ internal formal-model identifiers and a directory tree, which is developer-internal content. Users need to understand what formal verification guarantees and how to run it, with full details available via VERIFICATION_TOOLS.md link.

requirements.text: nForma.cjs provides a `--screenshot <module>` CLI mode that renders each TUI module headlessly as synthetic ANSI output, and bin/generate-tui-assets.cjs converts that output to Tokyo Night-themed SVG files in docs/assets/ via the `npm run assets:tui` script.
requirements.background: TUI documentation images previously required manual screenshots that drifted from the actual UI. This requirement ensures SVG assets are generated deterministically from live TUI module data, keeping documentation visuals in sync with the codebase.

requirements.text: `check-trace-schema-drift.cjs` detects schema drift and emits `check-results.ndjson`
Schema drift check runs as CI step in `run-formal-verify.cjs`
When circuit breaker is active, hook returns `hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision:'deny'` blocking Bash execution
Block reason names the oscillating file set, confirms circuit breaker is active, and lists allowed operations (read-only Bash)

Block reason instructs Claude to perform root cause analysis and map dependencies before resuming; explicitly instructs the user to manually commit the fix (since Claude cannot run git commit while blocked)
`never_observed` path entries in `validate-traces.cjs` evidence output carry `confidence: low|medium|high` based on trace volume and window duration
`observation_window` metadata (window_start, window_end, n_traces, n_events, window_days) written to `check-results.ndjson` for evidence-driven checks

`bin/git-heatmap.cjs` mines git history for numerical adjustments, bugfix hotspots, and churn ranking, producing `.planning/formal/evidence/git-heatmap.json` with multiplicative priority scoring and formal-coverage cross-reference for uncovered hot zones
requirements.background: High-churn and frequently-patched files without formal model coverage are blind spots for verification. This evidence artifact feeds nf:solve with empirical signal data so it can prioritize which files most need formal models.

requirements.text: bin/git-history-evidence.cjs classifies every commit into one of seven categories (feat/fix/refactor/docs/test/build/chore), cross-references changed files against TLA+ specifications via model-registry.json, identifies drift candidates where code evolved after its formal spec, and produces .planning/formal/evidence/git-history-evidence.json consumed by the nf-solve sweep pipeline as an informational residual
requirements.background: The existing git-heatmap evidence provides churn and bugfix signals but does not classify commits by type or detect when source files drift ahead of their TLA+ formal specs. This tool closes that gap, enabling nf-solve to surface files where code changed but the corresponding formal model is stale.

requirements.text: call-quorum-slot.cjs retries a failed slot call up to 2 times with exponential backoff (1s, 3s) before recording UNAVAIL in quorum-failures.json
providers.json contains explicit slot-to-provider mapping; when a provider probe returns DOWN, all slots on that provider skipped in a single dispatch decision

All formal verification tooling scripts (model generators, checkers, linkers, registry initializers in bin/) SHALL include @requirement annotations linking to the requirements they implement, and each SHALL have a corresponding test file with @req tracing.
requirements.background: 9 bin/ modules (formal-core, generate-formal-specs, check-liveness-fairness, etc.) and ~10 test files lack requirement tracing. This generalized requirement covers the entire formal verification toolchain rather than creating per-file requirements.

requirements.text: gate-a-grounding.cjs accepts a --base-ref <sha> flag that scopes grounding analysis to actions from files changed since the given commit, enforcing the 80% target independently on the scoped subset while reporting the global score as informational
requirements.background: Without diff scoping, the global grounding score can mask poor coverage of newly introduced code. The --base-ref flag enables CI and nf:solve to validate that new or modified features independently meet the grounding target, with graceful degradation to global mode when the instrumentation map is unavailable or no changed files have emission points.

requirements.text: The hook system provides three code-quality guardrails: a PostToolUse hook that auto-formats JS/TS files after Edit using prettier or biome (fail-open), a Stop hook that warns about leftover console.log statements in git-modified files without blocking, and a modular .claude/rules/ directory with project-specific convention files auto-loaded per session.
requirements.background: Adopted from everything-claude-code best practices to catch formatting drift at edit-time, debug logging at stop-time, and enforce project conventions automatically. All three mechanisms are fail-open and advisory.

requirements.text: After each deliberation round, system computes P(consensus | remaining rounds); if P < threshold (default 10%), escalation fires early instead of exhausting maxDeliberation
When verify-quorum-health detects P(consensus) < 95%, it recommends and auto-adjusts maxDeliberation in qgsd.json (with user approval)
`validate health` produces zero W005 false positives for versioned phase directories (`v0.X-YY-name` format)
`validate health` produces zero W007 false positives for versioned phase directories
`validate health` produces zero W002 false positives for versioned phase references in STATE.md

Load and filter baseline requirements by project profile (web/mobile/desktop/api/cli/library), present profile-filtered defaults during new-project and new-milestone workflows with opt-out controls, allowing users to deselect baseline requirements that don't apply
Installer detects no configured quorum agents and prompts user to run `/nf:mcp-setup`
QGSD's package.json pins GSD version — version lockstep ensures hook compatibility
Installer writes hooks to `~/.claude/settings.json` directly (not plugin.json hooks — stdout is silently discarded per GitHub #10225)
Installer adds UserPromptSubmit and Stop hook entries to `~/.claude/settings.json` hooks section
Installer performs validation before registering hooks: checks MCPs are configured in Claude Code settings, warns if Codex/Gemini/OpenCode not found
Installer is idempotent — running `npx qgsd@latest` again updates hooks and config without duplicating entries
Installer respects existing per-project `.claude/qgsd.json` overrides during updates
Installer registers PreToolUse circuit breaker hook in `~/.claude/settings.json` alongside existing hooks
Installer writes default `circuit_breaker` config block to qgsd.json on first install
Reinstall (idempotent) adds missing `circuit_breaker` config block without overwriting user-modified values

Bin scripts resolve ROOT from process.cwd() (the invoking project directory) rather than __dirname (the script install location). __dirname resolves to ~/.claude/ when installed, breaking all .planning/formal/ and .planning/ path lookups. The --project-root= flag provides an explicit override but the default must be process.cwd().
requirements.background: When scripts are installed to ~/.claude/bin/, __dirname becomes ~/.claude/bin/ and path.resolve(__dirname, ..) gives ~/.claude/ — not the users project directory. This caused R->F, F->T, and C->F layer transitions to report UNKNOWN in cross-repo usage.

requirements.text: Baseline requirements are merged into .planning/formal/requirements.json using an idempotent CLI tool that matches on text field to prevent duplicates and assigns next-available IDs per prefix namespace.
requirements.background: After baseline requirements are defined per project profile, the system needs a way to sync them into the formal requirements envelope without manual copy-paste and without creating duplicate entries on re-run.

requirements.text: User can set or update the API key for any agent through the wizard
Key is stored securely via keytar (bin/secrets.cjs)
Wizard writes key from keytar to `~/.claude.json` mcpServers env block during apply
Wizard automatically restarts the agent after key changes take effect
CI step detects liveness properties lacking a fairness declaration in `invariants.md` and emits `result=inconclusive` instead of `pass`
`run-formal-verify.cjs` STEPS includes a `ci:liveness-fairness-lint` step that enforces LIVE-01
PRISM always uses current scoreboard rates via `export-prism-constants` pre-step calibration
PostToolUse hook `qgsd-spec-regen.js` auto-regenerates TLA+/Alloy specs on XState machine changes
`sensitivity-sweep-feedback.cjs` detects threshold violations and triggers PRISM re-run
`propose-debug-invariants.cjs` mines TLA+ invariant candidates from debug sessions
Installer reads `~/.claude.json` to auto-detect MCP server names for Codex, Gemini, OpenCode
Detection matches server names containing "codex", "gemini", "opencode" (case-insensitive keyword match)
Detected names written to `~/.claude/qgsd.json` as `required_models` on install
If detection finds no matching servers, installer falls back to hardcoded defaults: `mcp__codex-cli__`, `mcp__gemini-cli__`, `mcp__opencode__`
User can manually edit `qgsd.json` to override detected names
Stop hook matches tool_use names by prefix (e.g. `mcp__codex-cli__` matches both `mcp__codex-cli__codex` and `mcp__codex-cli__review`)
`.planning/formal/spec/mcp-calls/` contains TLA+ model of MCP environment behavior
MCP availability rates from `run-prism.cjs` feed into the MCP environment model
`readMCPAvailabilityRates` exported with composite-key filter; `module.exports` always reachable via require.main guard; prism:mcp-availability STEPS entry and CI step added
GSD planning commands within this repo (new-project, plan-phase, etc.) auto-resolve questions via quorum before escalating to user
Only questions where quorum fails to reach consensus are presented to the user
Auto-resolved questions are presented as a list of assumptions before escalated questions
User can have multiple `claude-*` slots (`claude-1` through `claude-N`) each running a different model or provider
User can have multiple `copilot-N`, `opencode-N`, `codex-cli-N`, and `gemini-cli-N` slots as separate `~/.claude.json` entries
Adding a new slot of any family is supported by both direct config edit and via the mcp-setup wizard

Main TUI menu is organized into 3 modules (Agents, Requirements, Config) with a 6-character activity bar sidebar showing icons, user switches modules via F1/F2/F3 hotkeys or Tab/Shift+Tab cycling, and only module-relevant menu items are displayed at any time.
requirements.background: The flat 30+ item menu had become unwieldy and difficult to navigate. Grouping menu items into logical modules following the familiar VS Code activity bar pattern improves discoverability and reduces cognitive load when navigating the TUI.

requirements.text: Sessions module (F4) in nForma TUI embeds interactive Claude Code terminal sessions via blessed-xterm, supporting creation, connection, and termination of multiple PTY-backed sessions with full focus management and module switching.
requirements.background: Multi-session Claude Code management from within nForma requires an interactive terminal widget embedded in the TUI. blessed-xterm provides PTY-backed terminal embedding compatible with blessed widget system, enabling users to run multiple concurrent Claude Code sessions without leaving the TUI.

requirements.text: Sessions terminal widget uses pure-JavaScript @xterm/headless terminal emulation with child_process.spawn, eliminating native addon dependencies and enabling compatibility across all Node.js versions
requirements.background: The original blessed-xterm widget depends on node-pty, a native C++ addon causing ABI mismatch errors on Node.js upgrades. Replacing it with @xterm/headless (pure JavaScript ANSI/VT100 parser) and standard child_process.spawn eliminates the native addon requirement, making nforma portable across all Node versions without recompilation.

requirements.text: Sessions module persists active sessions to sessions.json across TUI restarts, restores session ID counters from persisted data, validates CWD existence before resume with fallback to process.cwd(), and prevents unwanted modal dispatch when switching to Sessions module
requirements.background: Without persistence, all session state was lost on TUI restart, forcing users to manually recreate sessions. The CWD guard prevents crashes when a previously-persisted session directory has been removed, and the modal fix eliminates a spurious New Session prompt triggered by module switching.

requirements.text: Each quorum round emits structured telemetry (slot, round, verdict, latency_ms, provider status) to a per-session log file
Scoreboard tracks quorum delivery rate -- percentage of calls that achieved target vote count (3/3 vs degraded 2/3)
Each slot gets a flakiness score from recent UNAVAIL/timeout frequency; high-flakiness slots deprioritized in dispatch ordering
Status shows recent UNAVAIL count per agent from quorum scoreboard
CLI tools exit with appropriate codes (0 = success, non-zero = failure) and write errors to stderr, output to stdout
Scan local project state for unfinished quick tasks, stale debug sessions, and unverified milestone phases; surface them as always-on issues in /nf:observe without requiring configuration.
requirements.background: Project housekeeping tasks—incomplete quick tasks, unresolved debug sessions, and active phases without verification—can fall through the cracks when only monitoring external sources (GitHub, Sentry). This requirement adds an internal work detector that automatically surfaces local consistency issues as observe issues, routing them to appropriate follow-up skills (/qgsd:quick, /qgsd:debug, /qgsd:solve) without manual configuration.

requirements.text: The analyze-assumptions CLI parses TLA+, Alloy, and PRISM formal models to extract assumptions, thresholds, and invariants, cross-references them against the debt ledger and observe handler registry, and outputs a gap report with proposed qgsd_-prefixed metrics and instrumentation snippets for every uncovered assumption.
requirements.background: Formal models encode critical system assumptions (e.g., max deliberation rounds, quorum thresholds) that must be monitored in production, but no mechanism existed to detect which assumptions lacked corresponding observe sources or debt entries. This script closes the observability loop by surfacing instrumentation gaps automatically.

requirements.text: The analyze-assumptions CLI classifies each extracted assumption into priority tiers (Tier 1: directly monitorable numeric constants, Tier 2: named invariants/assertions checkable via probes, Tier 3: structural constraints not runtime-observable), sorts gap reports by tier, supports --actionable filtering to Tier 1 only, and generates Prometheus gauge/histogram instrumentation snippets for Tier 1 assumptions.
requirements.background: Raw assumption lists from formal models are noisy — operators need to focus on the most actionable gaps first. Tiering by monitorability lets the observe skill prioritize numeric thresholds that can be directly instrumented with Prometheus metrics, while deferring structural constraints that require manual review.

requirements.text: Observe handlers return the standard schema { source_label, source_type, status: ok|error, issues: Array, error?: string } with all issue fields populated (id, title, severity, url, age, created_at, meta, source_type, issue_type). The dispatch layer validates the return shape before passing to the renderer.
requirements.background: Currently enforced by convention with no runtime validation. The renderer silently handles missing fields with fallbacks, which masks handler bugs. A schema check at dispatch catches issues before they reach the render pipeline.

requirements.text: Observe utility functions (formatAge, parseDuration, classifySeverity) are defined in a single canonical module and imported by all handlers. No handler duplicates these functions.
requirements.background: formatAge is duplicated in 6 files with 3 different null-handling behaviors (unknown, empty string, new vs future). parseDuration is duplicated in 2 files. This drift already causes visual inconsistency in observe output.

requirements.text: Upstream changes surfaced by observe are evaluated against existing code before porting. The system compares overlapping areas and classifies each as SKIP (our implementation is equivalent or better), CANDIDATE (upstream introduces something we lack), or INCOMPATIBLE (conflicts with our architecture). No upstream change is ported without this evaluation.
requirements.background: Even for tight fork parents, nForma may have intentionally diverged with better implementations. Blindly porting upstream changes would regress quality. The evaluation routing in observe.md implements SKIP/CANDIDATE/INCOMPATIBLE comparison.

requirements.text: Observe handlers catch all errors internally and return status: error with a descriptive error string. No handler throws — the dispatch layer Promise.allSettled is a safety net, not the primary error boundary.
requirements.background: Already followed by all 8+ handlers and enforced at the dispatch layer via Promise.allSettled, but not formalized. Critical for handler authors to know when adding new source types.

requirements.text: A machine-readable inventory classifies every non-test bin/ script as wired (reachable from a skill command, hook, or workflow) or lone (unreferenced), with purpose, classification, suggested integration point, and transitive dependency chains documented for each entry.
requirements.background: 46% of bin/ scripts were not reachable from any user-facing /nf: command, representing orphaned tooling with no discovery path. The inventory enables systematic integration of high-value standalone tools and identification of dead code candidates.

requirements.text: Observe handlers accept execFn and basePath options for dependency injection. All subprocess calls use the injected execFn (defaulting to execFileSync) and all filesystem paths resolve relative to basePath.
requirements.background: Handlers that shell out to git or read filesystem paths are hard to test in isolation without dependency injection. Accepting execFn and basePath as options enables unit tests to supply stubs and temporary directories.

requirements.text: Stateful observe handlers persist their cursor in .planning/ as JSON with a last_checked ISO8601 field. State writes use atomic write (write to temp file + rename) to prevent corruption.
requirements.background: Handlers that track incremental state (e.g., upstream commit cursors) need a durable, corruption-resistant persistence mechanism. Atomic write-then-rename prevents partial writes from corrupting the cursor file on crash or interrupt.

requirements.text: The observe handler surfaces nf:health diagnostic codes (E*/W*/I*) as observe issues with severity-mapped routing, gated to the QGSD source repo via core/bin/gsd-tools.cjs existence check.
requirements.background: Health check problems previously required manual /nf:health runs to discover. Wiring health diagnostics into /nf:observe as Category 15 creates a self-development feedback loop where planning health issues appear automatically in observe output, but only when running inside the QGSD repo itself.

requirements.text: When oscillation is detected and the oscillating file set contains only internal code files, Claude MUST enter oscillation resolution mode instead of hard-stopping
Oscillation resolution mode presents oscillation evidence (file set, commit graph) to all available quorum models with structural-coupling framing
Quorum deliberates (R3.3, up to 4 rounds) and may only approve unified solutions — partial/incremental fixes are rejected
On consensus, Claude presents the unified solution plan to the user for approval before any execution
If no consensus after 4 rounds, Claude hard-stops and escalates to the human with all model positions
`generate-proposed-changes.cjs` auto-synthesizes TLA+ deltas from PLAN.md truths
`run-phase-tlc.cjs` provides iterative TLC verification loop gating planning (3-attempt cap)
`quorum-formal-context.cjs` generates formal evidence blocks for quorum slots
QGSD installs and operates correctly on macOS, Ubuntu, and Windows without platform-specific workarounds
User can set quorum timeout (ms) per slot from a dedicated menu shortcut — not buried inside editAgent
User can configure update policy per slot: auto / prompt / skip
Auto-update policy check runs on manage-agents startup for slots configured as `auto`
User can export full roster config to a portable JSON file — all API key values replaced with `__redacted__` placeholders
User can import roster config from JSON file — validates schema, prompts to re-enter any redacted key, confirms before applying
Import creates a timestamped backup of `~/.claude.json` before applying any changes
The project shall be referred to as 'nForma' in all user-facing contexts
All skill shortcuts shall use the 'nf:' prefix (e.g., nf:solve, nf:quick)
User can change the base URL (provider) for an existing agent
Wizard offers curated provider list (AkashML, Together.xyz, Fireworks) + custom entry
Wizard updates `~/.claude.json` ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL and restarts agent on apply
User can select a named provider preset (AkashML / Together.xyz / Fireworks.ai) in addAgent/editAgent flow instead of manually typing a URL
User can clone an existing slot — copies provider URL and model config, prompts for new slot name
plan-milestone-gaps proposed gap closure phases are submitted to R3 quorum for approval before ROADMAP.md is updated (replaces AskUserQuestion confirmation gate)
execute-phase gaps_found triggers quorum diagnosis and auto-resolution (replaces chain halt + manual suggestion)
discuss-phase remaining user_questions (surviving R4 pre-filter) are routed to quorum in auto mode (replaces AskUserQuestion for gray areas)
A quorum round reaches consensus when all voting members return the same verdict AND none propose further improvements; no additional iteration is required once consensus is achieved

All quorum members (including Claude) MUST review prior-round positions from other voters and revise their own position before each deliberation round. No voter's position may be frozen across rounds — every participant re-evaluates after seeing peer arguments.
requirements.background: Fixes a behavioral gap where Claude's position was frozen at Round 1 and never revised during deliberation, creating an asymmetry where Claude could not be persuaded by BLOCK reasoning. This led to Claude writing solo rebuttals to override BLOCKs instead of dispatching proper deliberation rounds with cross-pollination.

requirements.text: checkpoint:human-verify in auto-mode triggers a quorum consensus gate requiring 100% APPROVE from all responding workers; any BLOCK vote or quorum unavailability escalates to the user instead of auto-approving.
requirements.background: Auto-mode previously auto-approved checkpoint:human-verify tasks, silently bypassing meaningful verification. The unanimous quorum gate closes this safety gap by requiring multi-model agreement that checkpoint criteria are met before proceeding, with fail-open escalation to the user.

requirements.text: `npx qgsd --reset-breaker` CLI flag clears `.claude/circuit-breaker-state.json` and logs confirmation — enables manual recovery when circuit breaker deadlocks due to blocked git commit
`check-trace-redaction.cjs` checks traces for PII/secret patterns and emits `check-results.ndjson`
Redaction check runs as CI step in `run-formal-verify.cjs`
Redaction violations emit `result=fail` (not warn)
Failures in external services (APIs, databases, third-party SDKs) are caught and handled gracefully — the application degrades functionality rather than crashing
Long-running operations (file uploads, data processing, API calls >2s) show progress indication and can be cancelled by the user
All hardcoded `get-shit-done/` path strings removed from `bin/gsd-tools.cjs`, workflow files, agent files, and template files
`--repair` cannot silently overwrite a rich STATE.md (>50 lines) without explicit `--force` flag; guard fires and repair is skipped with a message
Scoreboard tracks performance by slot name (`claude-1`, `copilot-1`) — slot is the stable key
Each scoreboard entry displays the current model loaded in that slot as context
When a slot's model changes, a new scoreboard row is created for that slot

`model-registry.json` entries gain a `requirements` array (string[]) listing the requirement IDs each model covers; existing entries seeded from the property-to-requirement map in TRACEABILITY_RESEARCH.md
Each verification runner (run-tlc.cjs, run-alloy.cjs, run-prism.cjs, CI lint steps) emits `requirement_ids` in its NDJSON output, extracted from model-registry or inline annotations
`requirements.json` envelope gains an optional `formal_models` array (string[]) per requirement, listing model file paths that verify it
Pre-commit hook runs secret scanning (e.g., Gitleaks) to block commits containing API keys, tokens, passwords, or credentials
CI pipeline runs deep secret scanning (e.g., TruffleHog) across full repo history on every PR
All external input (user input, API request bodies, query parameters, file uploads) is validated and sanitized at system boundaries before processing
Dependencies are scanned for known vulnerabilities in CI (e.g., npm audit, Dependabot, Snyk) and critical/high findings block merge

`bin/run-sensitivity-sweep.cjs` varies key model parameters (at minimum: quorum size N across its full range, timeout threshold T across low/medium/high values) across ≥3 values each, running TLA+/PRISM checks for each configuration, and records outcome deltas (pass→fail, pass→inconclusive transitions) in `.planning/formal/sensitivity-report.ndjson` using v2.1 schema — each record annotated with which parameter value caused the transition.

`plan-phase.md` step 8.3 (FV gate) is extended to also run `run-sensitivity-sweep.cjs` (fail-open) and inject `SENSITIVITY_CONTEXT` into the quorum `review_context`, surfacing the top-3 highest-impact parameters as planning recommendations: which code paths control them, which boundary values to test, and which metrics to monitor.

`bin/sensitivity-report.cjs` generates `.planning/formal/sensitivity-report.md` — a human-readable ranked list of sensitive parameters by outcome-flip count, each annotated with (a) the code path or configuration variable that controls it, (b) recommended unit/integration test cases for boundary values, (c) recommended monitoring metrics to track in production.
`detect-coverage-gaps.cjs` diffs TLC states vs conformance traces to produce `.planning/formal/coverage-gaps.md`
`generate-petri-net.cjs --roadmap` models phase dependencies as Petri net with critical path analysis
`prism-priority.cjs` ranks roadmap items by PRISM failure probability
`quorum-consensus-gate.cjs` gates quorum rounds by Poisson binomial consensus probability threshold
User sees all quorum agents referred to by slot name (`claude-1`, `copilot-1`, `gemini-cli-1`, etc.) in all QGSD output and commands
Migration script renames existing `~/.claude.json` mcpServers entries from model-based names to slot names automatically (non-destructive, invertible)
`mcp-status`, `mcp-set-model`, `mcp-update`, `mcp-restart` accept and display slot names correctly

Execute `/nf:solve` command to sweep all 7 layer transitions (Requirements→Formal, Formal→Tests, Code→Formal, Tests→Code, Formal→Code, Requirements→Docs, Docs→Code) and compute residual vectors showing gaps at each boundary, with optional auto-closing of gaps through iteration until convergence
requirements.background: The consistency solver provides a single command for diagnosing and fixing all verification layer gaps across the Requirements→Formal→Tests→Code stack. It replaces the need to manually run individual verification tools and cross-reference their outputs.

requirements.text: Parse all Alloy default constants from newline-separated constraint blocks and clear formalTestSyncCache at each solver iteration to ensure fresh residual computation after file mutations
requirements.background: The solver parseAlloyDefaults() was splitting constraint blocks on commas instead of newlines, missing 2 of 3 Alloy default constants. The formalTestSyncCache was never cleared between iterations, causing stale results after autoClose() mutations.

requirements.text: Solver discovers documentation files via config (docs_paths in .planning/config.json) with convention fallback (README.md, docs/**/*.md), checks R→D coverage by literal ID and keyword matching, and verifies D→C structural claims (file paths, CLI commands, dependencies) with false-positive filtering for template variables, example headings, home paths, and fenced code blocks
requirements.background: The solver had zero visibility into documentation consistency. A README could list commands that don't exist, reference deleted files, or omit shipped features — and /qgsd:solve would report all-green. R→D gaps are now auto-remediable (via SOLVE-04); D→C remains a manual-review-only layer that surfaces documentation drift.

requirements.text: Scope R→D gap detection to developer-category documentation files (docs/dev/) and automatically dispatch /nf:quick batches to generate requirement-to-documentation entries into docs/dev/requirements-coverage.md, while preserving user-controlled docs (docs/) as manual-only
requirements.background: The solver previously treated R→D gaps as informational-only with manual review required, and did not distinguish between user-authored docs (docs/) and auto-generatable developer docs (docs/dev/). This enhancement enables autonomous closure of R→D gaps by auto-generating developer documentation entries from requirements and source code, while ensuring user documentation remains human-controlled. R→D is now part of automatable_residual.

requirements.text: When qgsd:solve runs in another project, run-formal-verify.cjs discovers and verifies that project's formal models (from ROOT/.planning/formal/{tla,alloy,prism,petri}/) rather than hardcoding QGSD-internal model names. All child runners (run-tlc.cjs, run-alloy.cjs, run-prism.cjs, and variants) must honor --project-root for spec file resolution, JAR/tool lookup, and NDJSON output. QGSD-specific checks (XState machine extraction, provider roster constants) are gracefully skipped when their prerequisites do not exist in the target project.
requirements.background: The formal verification harness is hardcoded to QGSD's own models. When /qgsd:solve runs in another repo, it tries to verify QGSD-internal TLA+, Alloy, and PRISM models that don't exist there, producing 21+ false failures. The harness should be project-agnostic — discovering models from the target project's .planning/formal/ directory.

requirements.text: qgsd-solve diagnostic engine provides reverse traceability sweeps (C→R, T→R, D→R) that discover implementation artifacts without requirement backing, assembled via deduplication and acknowledged-not-required filtering, presented through a two-step candidate-then-approve pattern that prevents unbounded requirement expansion
requirements.background: Forward traceability (R→F→T→C→D) ensures requirements are implemented, but does not catch code, tests, or doc claims that exist without any requirement backing. Reverse sweeps close this gap by surfacing untraced artifacts as candidates, gated by human approval to prevent automated feedback loops from inflating the requirement set.

requirements.text: The solve F->T remediation pipeline SHALL provide pre-resolved context (requirement text, formal property definition, source file hints) alongside each test stub so executors can generate meaningful tests without codebase-wide search.
requirements.background: Without pre-resolved context, F->T executors produce empty stubs (assert.fail TODO). Pre-resolving context at stub generation time eliminates redundant codebase scanning across batch dispatches.

requirements.text: The solve orchestrator detects a legacy root-level .formal/ directory before the diagnostic sweep (Step 0), merges its contents into .planning/formal/ with canonical-wins conflict resolution, and proceeds fail-open if the migration script is missing or errors.
requirements.background: Projects that adopted formal verification before the .planning/formal/ consolidation may retain a root-level .formal/ directory. Without migration, the solver silently misses models and artifacts stored in the old location, causing incomplete diagnostics.

requirements.text: The solve diagnostic engine provides fast-mode iteration (--fast skips F->C and T->C layers), pattern-based D->C false-positive suppression via acknowledged-false-positives.json regex rules, auto-acknowledgement of Category B reverse discovery candidates, and recipe sidecars with absolute paths and pre-classified test templates.
requirements.background: The full solve loop took 30+ minutes per iteration and surfaced hundreds of false positives from prose word matches and Category B reverse candidates requiring unnecessary human review. These five improvements reduce iteration time to sub-second for file-parsing layers, suppress pattern-matched false positives, eliminate manual Category B triage, and provide executors with ready-to-use absolute paths and test boilerplate in recipe sidecars.

requirements.text: The T->C sweep collects line-level test coverage data and crossReferenceFormalCoverage() cross-references covered source files against formal-test-sync recipe source_files to identify false-green properties (tests pass but exercise zero implementing source files), surfacing an F->T->C coverage summary without altering residual scores, with coverage collection fail-open.
requirements.background: The T->C sweep previously only checked test pass/fail. Line-level coverage closes the full Formal->Test->Code traceability chain by detecting false greens — regardless of language or test framework.

requirements.text: The formal verification result schema SHALL distinguish infrastructure errors (binary not found, model missing, Java version wrong) from requirement violations (counterexample, divergence, property violation) using separate result values, and only requirement violations SHALL inflate the F->C residual.
nf:solve autoClose phase upgrades generated TODO test stubs to implementation-aware tests using recipe metadata before convergence.
requirements.background: Without auto-remediation, TODO stubs remain as assert.fail placeholders requiring manual intervention, leaving the formal-to-test gap unclosed after solve runs.

requirements.text: nf-solve persists a timestamped markdown session summary to .planning/formal/solve-sessions/ after every run, containing the full residual vector report, machine state JSON, and actions taken, with automatic pruning to retain only the 20 most recent session files
requirements.background: Solve session outputs (actionable items, config changes, unmapped events, fairness gaps) previously existed only in conversation context and were lost on /clear or compaction. Persisting them to disk enables multi-session continuity for gap closure without re-running diagnostics.

requirements.text: The nf:solve skill decomposes into three Agent-dispatched sub-skills (solve-diagnose, solve-remediate, solve-report) orchestrated by a thin dispatcher that retains only the convergence loop, report-only gate, and phase-routing logic.
requirements.background: The monolithic solve.md (971 lines) was loaded in full on every invocation, consuming excessive context. Decomposing into sub-skills reduces per-invocation context by 75-90% while preserving all diagnostic, remediation, and reporting logic via structured JSON input/output contracts with status-based branching.

requirements.text: Stop hook formalized in `QGSDStopHook.tla` + `MCStopHook.cfg`, TLC verifies safety + liveness
Quorum composition verified in `quorum-composition.als` — 3 composition rules hold
`generate-phase-spec.cjs` translates PLAN.md `must_haves: truths:` to TLA+ PROPERTY stubs

Formal scope scan uses per-module scope.json metadata with exact concept matching, source-file overlap, and module-name matching instead of substring-based keyword heuristics, and is centralized in bin/formal-scope-scan.cjs for all 4 workflow files
requirements.background: The previous inline keyword-substring matching in workflows produced false positives (e.g., session matching agent-loop, safety matching unrelated modules). Structured scope.json metadata per formal spec module with curated concepts enables precise semantic relevance matching without substring ambiguity.

requirements.text: All Alloy assertions verify non-tautological properties (no P=>P or x=x patterns), all TLA+ TypeOK variable domains use bounded ranges (no raw Nat/Int), and all Alloy check commands use per-sig scopes matching their signature hierarchy
requirements.background: Audit of 94 formal models (61 Alloy, 33 TLA+) revealed tautological assertions giving false verification confidence, unbounded TLA+ counters risking state space explosion, and Alloy check commands using overall scopes that under-explore signature combinations.

requirements.text: audit-milestone updates STATE.md "Stopped at" and "Current Position" fields with the audit result (passed / gaps_found / tech_debt) after writing the MILESTONE-AUDIT.md artifact
State schema: `{ active, file_set[], activated_at, commit_window_snapshot[] }` — captures what triggered the breaker
Hook reads existing state first — if active, applies enforcement immediately without re-running git log detection
State file created silently if absent; failure to write logs to stderr but never blocks execution

execute-plan Route C (all phases complete) chains into /nf:audit-milestone with gap-closure detection instead of suggesting /nf:complete-milestone directly, matching transition.md Route B logic for both yolo (auto-invoke) and interactive (suggest) modes.
requirements.background: Previously, when the last plan of the last phase completed, execute-plan Route C suggested /nf:complete-milestone directly, bypassing the mandatory milestone audit step. Route C now mirrors transition.md Route B to ensure audits are always enforced.

requirements.text: phase-complete falls back to ROADMAP.md heading parsing when no next-phase directory exists on disk, using segment-aware version comparison for versioned phase IDs (e.g., v0.28-01 vs v0.28-02), and sets is_last_phase=true only when the roadmap contains no higher-numbered phase
requirements.background: Previously, phase-complete relied solely on disk directory scanning to detect the next phase, causing false is_last_phase=true when the next phase existed in ROADMAP.md but had no directory yet. This broke the auto-advance chain (Route A) by incorrectly triggering milestone-complete (Route B). The fix also replaces parseFloat-based comparison with segment-aware splitting, since parseFloat('0.28-01') and parseFloat('0.28-02') both yield 0.28.

requirements.text: gemini-mcp-server npm package name is unscoped (`gemini-mcp-server`, not `@tuannvm/gemini-mcp-server`) — `~/.claude.json` mcpServers["gemini-cli"].args reflects the unscoped name
Stop hook reads transcript JSONL for tool_use entries matching configured quorum model names
Stop hook checks `stop_hook_active` flag first — if true, exits 0 immediately (infinite loop prevention)
Stop hook checks `hook_event_name` — if `SubagentStop`, exits 0 immediately (subagent exclusion)
Stop hook scopes transcript search to current turn only (lines since last user message boundary) — survives context compaction

Stop hook reads transcript JSONL as the authoritative source of quorum evidence — no fast-path pre-check (design decision: last_assistant_message substring matching is not a reliable signal; JSONL parse is synchronous and correct for all transcript sizes)
Stop hook verifies quorum only when a configured planning command was issued in the current turn (scope filtering)
Stop hook blocks with `{"decision": "block", "reason": "..."}` when quorum is missing — reason includes exact tool names and instructions

Block reason message format: "QUORUM REQUIRED: Before completing this /nf:[command] response, call [tool1], [tool2], [tool3] with your current plan. Present their responses, then deliver your final output."
Stop hook passes (exits 0, no decision field) when quorum evidence found or no planning command in scope
QGSD ships as separate npm package (`qgsd` or `get-shit-done-quorum`) that wraps GSD
When GSD releases a new planning command, QGSD releases a patch update adding the command to the default `quorum_commands` list
QGSD changelog explicitly tracks which GSD version it is compatible with
No QGSD code modifies any GSD source files — all additions are in separate files (`hooks/qgsd-stop.js`, `hooks/qgsd-prompt.js`, `bin/qgsd-install.js`)

`bin/generate-traceability-matrix.cjs` reads model-registry, requirements.json, and check-results.ndjson to produce `.planning/formal/traceability-matrix.json` with property-level links between requirements and formal properties

The traceability matrix includes a `coverage_summary` section: total requirements, covered count, coverage percentage, list of uncovered requirements, list of orphan properties (properties with no requirement mapping)
The traceability matrix is generated as a step in `run-formal-verify.cjs` after all checks complete
Bidirectional validation detects asymmetric links (model claims requirement X but requirement X does not claim that model) and emits warnings
CI guard warns when formal coverage percentage drops below a configurable threshold (default 15%) compared to the previous traceability matrix

The /nf:formal-test-sync command cross-references formal model invariants with unit test coverage, validates formal constants against runtime config defaults, reports coverage gaps, generates test stubs, and integrates unit test coverage into the traceability matrix
Conformance tracing infrastructure (schema definition, divergence attribution, trace validation) SHALL be requirement-traced and have test coverage with @req annotations.
requirements.background: 2 conformance tracing modules (conformance-schema, attribute-trace-divergence) and ~4 related test files lack requirement tracing. Generalizes coverage for the trace validation subsystem.

requirements.text: mapToXStateEvent normalizes event identity via event.action || event.type fallback and handles quorum_fallback_t1_required, quorum_block_r3_2, and security_sweep actions — non-FSM actions in KNOWN_NON_FSM_ACTIONS are counted as valid (not divergent)
requirements.background: The conformance trace validator reported 6373 false divergences because mapToXStateEvent only read event.action, but 5649 events used event.type instead, and two additional actions lacked switch cases.

requirements.text: The formal proximity index builder reads all 12 formal artifact types and emits a bidirectional adjacency graph (proximity-index.json) with reverse edges for every forward edge, and the query CLI provides reach, path, neighbors, impact, coverage, proximity, and stats commands over that graph.
requirements.background: Isolated formal-to-code lookups required manual cross-referencing across 12 separate artifact files. The proximity index unifies these into a single bidirectional graph with BFS traversal and proximity scoring.

requirements.text: `bin/generate-triage-bundle.cjs` reads `check-results.ndjson` and writes `.planning/formal/diff-report.md` (per-check delta from last run) and `.planning/formal/suspects.md` (checks with `result=fail` or `triage_tags` set)
`run-formal-verify.cjs` calls `generate-triage-bundle.cjs` as the final step after all checks complete
All FV checkers append normalized JSON to `check-results.ndjson` — `{ tool, check_id, result, detail, ts }`
`run-formal-verify.cjs` generates `check-results.ndjson` as canonical output artifact
Triage bundle reads from `check-results.ndjson`, not tool stdout; CI enforcement steps run inside orchestrator before summary read (v0.19-11 timing fix)
CI step exits non-zero when any `result=fail` entry exists in `check-results.ndjson`

A UPPAAL timed automaton model (`.planning/formal/uppaal/quorum-races.xml`) captures the concurrency structure of the quorum protocol — specifically when concurrent slot responses and timeout expirations fire relative to each other. The model uses `runtime_ms` bounds from `check-results.ndjson` as empirical timing constraints (clock guards and invariants), not hardcoded constants.

`bin/run-uppaal.cjs` executes the UPPAAL model checker (verifyta CLI) against `quorum-races.xml` and writes a check result to `check-results.ndjson` using the v2.1 schema (SCHEMA-01 prerequisite). The STEPS entry `uppaal:quorum-races` is added to `run-formal-verify.cjs`.

The model surfaces at least two critical measurement points as annotated properties: (a) the minimum inter-slot response gap that prevents a race condition, (b) the maximum timeout value for which the quorum can still reach consensus before the planning gate deadline.
UserPromptSubmit hook detects GSD planning commands via explicit allowlist regex match against prompt field
Allowlist contains exactly 6 commands: new-project, plan-phase, new-milestone, discuss-phase, verify-work, research-phase
UserPromptSubmit hook injects quorum instructions via `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` (not systemMessage — goes into Claude's context window)
Injected context names the exact MCP tools to call and instructs Claude to present model responses before delivering final output
UserPromptSubmit hook never fires on execute-phase or other non-planning commands
Every user-initiated action produces immediate feedback (loading/disabled state) and completion feedback (result message, navigation change, or visible state update)
Destructive actions (delete, reset, remove, overwrite) require explicit confirmation or provide undo within a reasonable window
Error messages are human-readable, explain what went wrong, and suggest a next step or recovery action
`qgsd-verifier` agent runs `run-formal-verify` after implementation and includes `check-results.ndjson` digest in `VERIFICATION.md`
`VERIFICATION.md` template gains a `## Formal Verification` section summarizing pass/fail/warn counts per formalism (tla, alloy, prism, ci)
Formal model runners (Alloy, TLA+, PRISM, UPPAAL) MUST execute in headless mode — no GUI windows or AWT initialization. For Java-based tools, `-Djava.awt.headless=true` MUST be passed before `-jar`.
requirements.background: On macOS, Java-based formal tools (especially Alloy) initialize AWT/Swing GUI even in CLI exec mode unless explicitly told not to. This causes unexpected GUI windows during automated qgsd:solve runs.

requirements.text: All JVM-spawning formal model runners (TLC and Alloy) MUST include -Xms64m and -Xmx heap cap flags before -jar, defaulting to 512MB and configurable via QGSD_JAVA_HEAP_MAX env var, and run-formal-verify.cjs MUST execute tool groups sequentially by default with --concurrent opt-in.
requirements.background: The formal verification suite spawns up to 14 JVM processes across 5 tool groups. Without -Xmx caps each JVM claims ~25% of physical RAM, and concurrent execution via Promise.all causes 5+ JVMs competing for 125%+ RAM, leading to swap thrashing or OOM kills. Sequential-by-default execution with heap caps limits peak memory to one capped JVM at a time.

requirements.text: Planner agent includes a System Integration Awareness section that requires wiring tasks for new artifacts, and verifier agent includes a dedicated Orphaned Producer Check step (Step 5.5) that flags new files with no system-level consumer as verification gaps.
requirements.background: Plans could create new artifacts (bin/ scripts, hooks, workflows) with tests but no system-level consumer, leaving orphaned producers in the codebase. This requirement ensures planners explicitly plan wiring tasks and verifiers catch orphaned producers as first-class gaps.

requirements.text: `/nf:health` surfaces W008 warning when a quorum slot has ≥3 failures recorded in `quorum-failures.json`
User can run `/nf:mcp-setup` to start the MCP configuration wizard
First run (no configured agents) presents a guided linear onboarding flow step by step
Re-run shows the current agent roster as a navigable menu
Each agent in the menu shows current model, provider, and key status (present/missing)
User confirms before changes are applied; wizard restarts affected agents after apply
`/nf:mcp-setup` re-run menu includes an "Edit Quorum Composition" option
Composition screen shows all discovered slots with on/off toggle for `quorum.active` inclusion
User can add a new slot for any family (claude, copilot, opencode, codex-cli, gemini-cli) from within the wizard, which writes the new entry to `~/.claude.json` and triggers restart

User can run /nf:settings to access a guided project manager hub with state-aware dashboard (milestone, progress, phase, config summary) and categorized main menu (Continue Working, Project Management, Configuration, Quick Task) with smart routing and profile/baselines management
requirements.background: The project manager hub unifies QGSD's core capabilities (status awareness, action routing, configuration) into a single entry point. Previously /qgsd:progress showed status and routing while /qgsd:settings showed only configuration; the hub merges both experiences with additional project profile and baseline management. The --config flag preserves backward compatibility.
